item.title: X-ray technician shortage a disaster for Health Ministry, no timeline for Korovou Hospital – Ravunawa
item.description: While responding, Assistant Minister for Health Penioni Ravunawa admits that the shortage of radiology staff is a disaster for the Ministry and has been a challenge since the Coalition Government came into office. Ravunawa says under the previous TELS and Toppers scholarship schemes, university graduates could easily leave the country after completing their studies.
item.content:encoded:
While responding, Assistant Minister for Health Penioni Ravunawa admits that the shortage of radiology staff is a disaster for the Ministry and has been a challenge since the Coalition Government came into office.

Ravunawa says under the previous TELS and Toppers scholarship schemes, university graduates could easily leave the country after completing their studies.

He says the Coalition Government has since introduced a bond system to help retain skilled workers in Fiji.

Ravunawa says training for radiology and laboratory technicians at the Fiji National University is accredited in Australia and New Zealand, meaning graduates can seek employment in those countries soon after completing their studies.

He says under the bond system, students who complete four years of training are required to serve Fiji for at least six years before they can leave, unless they repay the full cost of their bond.

Ravunawa says the Ministry of Health is also introducing a buffer system across its workforce to retain senior officers and strengthen staffing levels.

The Assistant Minister further says a new floor-mounted x-ray machine, supported by artificial intelligence, is also planned for Korovou Hospital.

Ravunawa says the machine is already in Suva but requires infrastructure upgrades, including a three-phase power supply, before it can be installed.

He says work is underway to improve the space and upgrade the electricity supply for the installation of the machine.

item.title: 7 Miles crash causes congestion through Nakasi and Laqere
item.description: A two-vehicle collision at 7 Miles this morning is causing heavy traffic congestion for drivers travelling through Nakasi, Laqere and surrounding areas. The Fiji Police Force is yet to confirm details of the crash, including whether there were any injuries or fatalities.
item.content:encoded:
A two-vehicle collision at 7 Miles this morning is causing heavy traffic congestion for drivers travelling through Nakasi, Laqere and surrounding areas.

The Fiji Police Force is yet to confirm details of the crash, including whether there were any injuries or fatalities.

Speaking to fijivillage News, Sawani resident Rohit Sharma, who was caught in the traffic, says the collision involved two vehicles and the damage appeared to be extensive.

Sharma says he had left home at around 6.30am and made a stop in Nadawa before continuing his journey.

He said that by 7.48am, he was still stuck in traffic as police and other authorities attended the scene.

Drivers are advised to expect delays and exercise caution while travelling through the area.

item.title: Classroom blocks at Tailevu North College damaged in tornado in 2020 yet to be rebuilt
Three people arrested following Cunningham robbery
item.description: Priya Nand , Antornette Vuniuca · 04/07/2026 Three people have been arrested in connection with a robbery case in Cunningham yesterday morning.
item.content:encoded:
Priya Nand , Antornette Vuniuca · 04/07/2026

Three people have been arrested in connection with a robbery case in Cunningham yesterday morning.

Police say the incident was captured on CCTV.

They say initial robbery investigation led to information about the alleged involvement of two other people, who were also arrested by Valelevu Police.

Police say the three suspects are also alleged to be linked to other robbery cases in the area.

They say the suspects remain in custody at the Valelevu Police Station as investigations continue.

Cunningham Stage 1 resident Sheetal Chand had told fijivillage News that she was robbed in her driveway while leaving for work early in the morning.

Chand said the incident happened at about 6.30am when a man opened the rear door of her vehicle, grabbed her handbag and lunch bag, and ran off as she was reversing out of her driveway.

item.title: Court to rule on July 14th on bail application of man accused of breaking into RFMF armoury
item.description: The Suva High Court will make its ruling on 14th July on the baill application of the man who is alleged to have broken into the Republic of Fiji Military Forces armoury at the Queen Elizabeth Barracks. Isaia Ledua is charged with one count of aggravated burglary and one count of damaging property.
item.content:encoded:
The Suva High Court will make its ruling on 14th July on the baill application of the man who is alleged to have broken into the Republic of Fiji Military Forces armoury at the Queen Elizabeth Barracks.

Isaia Ledua is charged with one count of aggravated burglary and one count of damaging property.

He appeared before Justice Dane Tuiqereqere this afternoon.

Ledua's counsel Jerry Dinati stated in court today that the charges against his client are defective.

Dinati says looking at the charges, there is no direct evidence disclosed that reveals his client was at QEB.

He also says that his client was charged while Jone Vakarisi was at the Barracks as a cover up.

State counsel, Shahil Kumar says they will prosecute and fight the case in trial.

Justice Tuiqereqere told Dinati that his client has to go back to remand as the prosecution has to respond to his bail application.

item.title: Cunningham woman robbed while leaving for work
item.description: A woman from Cunningham Stage 1 is calling for better security after she was robbed in her driveway while leaving for work early this morning. Speaking to fijivillage News, Sheetal Chand says the incident happened at about 6.30am after she opened her gate and got into her vehicle.
item.content:encoded:
A woman from Cunningham Stage 1 is calling for better security after she was robbed in her driveway while leaving for work early this morning.

Speaking to fijivillage News, Sheetal Chand says the incident happened at about 6.30am after she opened her gate and got into her vehicle.

She says as she was reversing, a man opened the rear door, grabbed her handbag and lunch bag, and ran off.

Police have not identified any suspect or laid any charges in relation to the incident.

Chand says she reported the matter to the Cunningham Police Station before taking CCTV footage to the Nabua Police Station.

She says Police and forensic officers later visited her home, examined her vehicle, inspected the scene and reviewed the CCTV footage.

Police are yet to comment on the incident.

A woman from Cunningham Stage 1 is calling for better security after she was robbed in her driveway while leaving for work early this morning. Speaking to fijivillage News, Sheetal Chand says the incident happened at about 6.30am after she opened her gate and got into her vehicle.

item.title: Work begins on Fiji's largest solar farm at Nadi International Airport
item.description: Construction has officially begun on the Nadi International Airport Solar Farm, which is expected to become the largest solar farm in Fiji. Speaking at the groundbreaking ceremony, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Tourism and Civil Aviation Viliame Gavoka says the 3.25-megawatt project will help strengthen Fiji's renewable energy efforts while improving energy security.
item.content:encoded:
Construction has officially begun on the Nadi International Airport Solar Farm, which is expected to become the largest solar farm in Fiji.

Speaking at the groundbreaking ceremony, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Tourism and Civil Aviation Viliame Gavoka says the 3.25-megawatt project will help strengthen Fiji's renewable energy efforts while improving energy security.

Gavoka says the first phase of the project will power airport operations with renewable energy, reduce reliance on traditional energy sources, lower operating costs and reduce Fiji Airports' carbon footprint.

He says Nadi International Airport is Fiji's main gateway to the world, and it is important that it has reliable and modern infrastructure.

Gavoka says the project supports Fiji's long-term commitment to renewable energy and sustainable development.

He also highlighted Fiji Airports' recent achievements, including receiving the 2026 Green Airports Recognition Platinum Award and being named the Best Airport in the South Pacific earlier this year.

Gavoka says the solar farm has been designed to meet aviation safety standards, and thanked Fiji Airports and its project partners for helping deliver the development.

item.title: Mother pleads not guilty to manslaughter charge over toddler's death
item.description: A woman charged in relation to the death of her two-year-old son has pleaded not guilty in the Lautoka High Court. Vaseva Kabu is charged with one count of manslaughter arising from breach of duty.
item.content:encoded:
A woman charged in relation to the death of her two-year-old son has pleaded not guilty in the Lautoka High Court.

Vaseva Kabu is charged with one count of manslaughter arising from breach of duty.

It is alleged that on 18th October 2017 in Tavua, Kabu neglected to supervise her toddler while travelling on a bus.

The Office of the Director of Public Prosecution alleges the child put his head outside the bus window and became trapped between the bus and an Energy Fiji Limited post, resulting in his death.

The State and the defence have been directed to resolve any pre-trial issues before the next court date.

The matter has been adjourned to 16th July for a pre-trial conference, while Kabu's bail has been extended.

item.title: Bail granted to 4 police officers charged with alleged assault during drug raid
item.description: Four police officers charged with assault during a drug raid in Wailea in 2024 have been granted bail by the Suva Magistrates Cour

item.title: Commission hears calls for greater powers for Permanent Secretaries and mandatory investment of resource income in USP submission
item.description: Greater decision-making powers should be returned to Permanent Secretaries instead of being concentrated in the hands of Ministers

item.title: Interlink calls for urgent action and allow foreign seafarers to ensure the safety of the travelling public
item.description: An urgent call is being made by Interlink Shipping Services Managing Director, Ashish Kumar to the government and the Maritime Saf

item.title: Malimali and Savu recommend FICAC reform, removing immunity, and strengthening rights
item.description: Suva lawyers Barbara Malimali and Samuela Savu have proposed several changes to Fiji's Constitution, including stripping FICAC of
